alerts.title: Suspicious Large Transaction: ₹99,999 Bank Account Update
alerts.description: A single transaction of ₹99,999 from merchant 'View: Account Update For Your HDFC Bank' was recorded. This description pattern is commonly associated with phishing or fraudulent bank notification scams — legitimate banks do not debit accounts for 'account updates'. Verify this transaction immediately with HDFC Bank.

alerts.title: Unclassified 'Others' Category Dominates Spending at ₹5,69,909
alerts.description: ₹5,69,909 (nearly 100% of this month's total spend of ₹5,69,911) falls under the 'Others' category, which is ₹3,79,939 above the 3-month average of ₹1,89,970. This extreme concentration of unclassified spend makes budgeting and fraud detection very difficult.

alerts.title: 47 UPI Transactions Totalling ₹4,69,910 with Vague Merchant Name
alerts.description: The top merchant entry '❗ You Have Done A UPI Txn. Check Detail' accounts for 47 transactions totalling ₹4,69,910 — averaging ₹9,998 per transaction. This generic description suggests these transactions are either unresolved UPI references or potentially unauthorised; each should be individually verified in your UPI app.

alerts.title: Possible Fraudulent Credit Card Debit of ₹2
alerts.description: A transaction of ₹2 from 'A Payment Was Made Using Your Credit Car' is a classic penny-test charge used by fraudsters to verify a card is active before making larger unauthorised charges. Cross-check your credit card statement and report to your bank if unrecognised.

tips: Immediately call HDFC Bank on their official helpline (1800-202-6161) to verify the ₹99,999 'Account Update' transaction — if fraudulent, file a dispute within 30 days to maximise chargeback chances.

Review all 47 UPI transactions averaging ₹9,998 each (total ₹4,69,910) in your UPI app (BHIM/GPay/PhonePe) by checking the merchant VPA for each — report any unrecognised payees to your bank and NPCI at 18001201740.

Block or freeze your credit card linked to the ₹2 'Payment Made Using Your Credit Car' test charge immediately via your bank's app to prevent larger fraudulent charges that may follow such penny-testing attempts.

Request your bank to apply proper merchant name tagging or use a UPI app with detailed transaction categorisation — currently 99.9% of your ₹5,69,911 spend is in 'Others', making it impossible to track or budget by category.

Set a UPI transaction alert and daily cap (recommended: ₹25,000/day for personal use) via your bank's net banking portal — this month's average UPI transaction of ₹9,998 across 47 transactions suggests high-value transfers that warrant additional authentication controls.
